rule: Ecommerce sites with checkout and admin management usually start above simple brochure sites.
Small business websites with 4-6 pages and contact forms are mid-sized fixed-scope projects.
Membership, gated content, subscriptions, and auth significantly increase scope.
Custom SaaS tools with auth, dashboards, roles, data workflows, admin controls, deployment, and QA are application builds, not brochure websites.
Document parser and AI extraction SaaS MVPs need pricing room for upload flows, parsing accuracy work, review UI, validation, exports, privacy, and edge-case testing.
Single-platform mobile MVPs without heavy backend needs are moderate projects.
Marketplace apps with multiple roles, tracking, payments, and admin are large builds.
Social media MVPs with profiles, feeds, media uploads, engagement, and moderation are large freelance builds but should be phased before enterprise scale.
Chat apps with real-time messaging, media attachments, and notifications require backend reliability and phased scope.

Mobile game MVPs with gameplay mechanics, levels, art integration, scoring, monetization, app-store QA, and optional leaderboard support are large freelance builds and must be scoped by content count.
Mobile UI design is often priced by screen count plus prototype and handoff effort.
Dashboard redesigns require information architecture and complex states, so screen count alone is not enough.
Recurring SEO content should price research, outlines, writing, editing, and revision separately.
Dashboards depend heavily on data cleanliness, number of sources, refresh automation, and KPI definition.
Pakistan/local pricing preference should lower USD ranges for local-market competitiveness.
